title: From a kidnapping in France to crypto wallet in Venezuela: Inside a ransom laundering scheme
subhead: An investigation into the kidnapping of a cryptocurrency entrepreneur's father has uncovered an unexpected criminal network, highlighting the complexity of the financial channels criminals use to launder money.

title: 'It was 30°C in the classrooms this afternoon, it was very tough': France experiences its hottest day in May on record
Israeli strikes kill at least 31 in south Lebanon as Israel expands ground operations
Economists Gabriel Zucman and Emmanuel Saez, the two 'Frenchies' campaigning to tax the fortunes of California's billionaires
subhead: California's 250 billionaires have amassed staggering wealth while paying very little in taxes, the two French researchers criticized in an op-ed for the 'New York Times.' They support a proposal for a one-time 5% wealth tax, which will be put to a referendum on November 3.

title: Sweden's Greta Thunberg and her fellow climate activists balance hope and despair
subhead: For seven years, filmmaker Helena Molin followed a dozen teenagers who helped found the Fridays for Future movement in 2018. The resulting documentary has been met with critical acclaim.

title: Roland-Garros 2026: Loïs Boisson's comeback after long injury ends in early exit
subhead: Defeated in straight sets (2-6, 2-6) by Russia's Anna Kalinskaya in the opening round, the French player, in far from her best form, could not recapture the magic that had carried her to the semi-finals of the 2025 tournament.

title: 'Believing they already serve humanity, Musk, Bezos and Thiel see no need for philanthropy'
subhead: With his foundation, Bill Gates embodied philanthropy. But his ties to Jeffrey Epstein have tarnished his reputation, and the new tech tycoons now place more faith in themselves than in governments to improve humanity, writes Le Monde's Arnaud Leparmentier in his column.

French airlines are finally turning to sustainable fuels to break free from fossil fuel dependence
subhead: Sustainable aviation fuels, which do not require any extraction of fossil carbon, are now being seen as an opportunity by airlines. But they remain extremely expensive and the available supply is insufficient.

title: How China uses influencers to polish its image
subhead: Chongqing, a futuristic Chinese city, has caught the eye of influencers and bloggers. Beijing, meanwhile, welcomes new visitors, often unaware of the geopolitical and ideological currents running beneath their social media posts.

title: The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
subhead: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.
